sections.paragraph:
not used in Kal. Arab. بَلَجَ to be bright, to shine forth as the dawn. V. to laugh, to be cheerful, from the idea of a bright countenance.

Hiphil-

(1) to cause to shine forth. Amo 5:9, הַמַּבְלִיג שֹׁד עַל־עָז “causing desolation to shine forth upon the mighty,” i.e. suddenly bringing it upon them; a metaphor taken from the dawn quickly and suddenly spreading itself, compare Joe 2:2.

(2) to make cheerful, sc. the countenance, to be made cheerful, Psalm 39:14 Job 9:27 10:20 . Hence מַבְלִיגִית, and-
